user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp
## My request for Codex:
implement `lib\FlyGuiViews\ModalDialog.cpp` and `lib\FlyGuiViews\ModalDialog.h`, which is a `FlyGuiView`.
Configure it with a sprite, the sprite dimensions, the sprite's position will be automatically determined for being at the top of the view (avoiding the top bar) and centered.
Configuration also sets a text to be displayed.

Configuration also sets a "next view" to transition to when dismissed. Sometimes it will be the previous view, sometimes it will be the home view. Since configuring happens before showing the view, the caller can easily just use `getCurrentView` if they want.
The text should probably start at y=130, but... left aligned yet centered, the text should accept '\n' and render new lines correctly
The bottom of the display should say "press anywhere to continue". And that should be the behavior.
It's internal onunload should clear all of these configured parameters to avoid the next call having lingering unexpected behaviors

`RecordingView` should use this view to confirm that a recording is finished, using `sprit_thumbsup_100` as the sprite, the text saying "<file name>\nhas been recorded", before transitioning to the main view
agent_reply: Implemented `ModalDialog` as a `FlyGuiView` and hooked it into the recording completion flow.
- Registered the view and exposed `get_modal_dialog()` in [all_init.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:1)
- Configurable sprite, dimensions, text, and next view.
- Sprite is centered below the top bar.
- Text starts at `y=130`, left aligned in a centered text area, with `\n` supported.
- Touch anywhere or press any hardware button dismisses and transitions to the configured next view.
- `onUnload()` clears the configuration to avoid stale state.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest`; build passed.

agent_reply: - level-2-only tamper code in the bottom-right, using a larger font.
